headline: State assemblies average only 18 sitting days per year
context: Malaysia's 13 state legislative assemblies averaged only 18 sitting days per year in 2025, with Perlis sitting for just 7 days and Pahang for only 9 days.

cards.big: State assemblies sit 18 days a year on average — Perlis manages just 7
cards.sub: State legislators work fewer days than most Malaysians take annual leave

cards.big: No state constitution mandates minimum sitting days for legislative assemblies
cards.sub: Menteri Besar and Chief Ministers control calendars with unchecked discretion

cards.big: 13 state assemblies cost combined RM340 million annually to operate
cards.sub: At 18 days average, each sitting day costs RM1.4 million across all states

cards.big: Selangor leads with 42 sitting days — 6 times more than Perlis
cards.sub: Vast disparity shows state governance quality depends entirely on political will

cards.big: A legislature that sits 7 days a year isn't governing — it's rubber-stamping the executive's agenda
Should all state assemblies be constitutionally required to sit at least 30 days per year?
